item.title: Supreme Court sides with Monsanto in case over cancer risks from weedkiller
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that Monsanto cannot be held liable under state laws for failing to warn consumers about the alleged cancer risks of its weedkiller Roundup on its label.

item.title: Big Tech is all in on AI. Now all they need is customers.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: Are deadly heat waves driving Europeans to embrace air conditioning?
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump strip deportation protections from Syrians, Haitians
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections.

item.title: Poland's deputy prime minister warns of possible Russian "false flag operation"
item.description: Poland's deputy prime minister tells CBS News he "wouldn't exclude the Russians doing some kind of false flag operation" to justify an attack on NATO.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski warns Russia could launch a "false flag operation"
item.description: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski said Russia could launch a "false flag operation" to try and justify an attack on a U.S. ally. "I wouldn't exclude the Russians doing some kind of false flag operation against Russian territory in order to have a pretext for hitting one of the NATO countries," Sikorski told CBS News' Aidan Stretch.

item.title: Is a central air conditioning unit worth buying?
item.description: Wondering if installing central air is worth the cost? Here's how to determine if it's the best move for your home.

item.title: Fed's preferred inflation gauge hits 3-year high
item.description: Inflation continued to rise in May, with the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index rising at an annual rate of 4.1%.

item.title: $45,000 CD vs. $45,000 high-yield savings account: Which can earn more over the next year?
item.description: Interest earnings with each account type will be similar, but they won't be identical. Here's what to know now.

item.title: U.S. moves forward to World Cup knockout rounds after victories against Paraguay and Australia
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team clinched a lead spot in their World Cup group after wins against Paraguay and Australia. CBS News' Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii law restricting guns on some private property
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down a Hawaii restriction that prohibits concealed-carry permit holders from bringing their firearms onto private property that is open to the public, like gas stations, restaurants or shops.

item.title: A massive tornado devastated this small city 50 years ago. It's become a symbol of resilience
item.description: Xenia is a small city in Ohio that was devastated by a powerful tornado more than 50 years ago. But the community rebounded and rebuilt. Mark Strassmann shows how it has become a symbol of perseverance.

item.title: DeSantis to speak at "Alligator Alcatraz" amid reports facility is closing
item.description: Conditions at "Alligator Alcatraz" were criticized by lawyers, families and human rights groups who claimed detainees were mistreated.

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: Behind the apparent rise of democratic socialism and what it could mean for U.S. politics
item.description: The three candidates New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed in New York won their congressional primaries this week. Last November, Mamdani won the mayoral race after running on a democratic socialist platform. "CBS Mornings" takes a deeper dive into what appears to be rising support for democratic socialism.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Oil prices keep dropping as Oman rules out Strait of Hormuz "transit fees"
item.description: With the U.S.-Iran agreement appearing to hold, Oman rules out future Strait of Hormuz "transit fees" and oil prices continue their fall.

item.title: Trump demands "loyalty" from allies in meeting with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, leveling criticisms against European allies for not supporting the U.S. war with Iran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro breaks down their meeting.

item.title: How far can the U.S. go in the World Cup?
item.description: The U.S. will take on Turkey on Thursday for the team's final match in the group stage. With two wins, they've already earned their spot in the next round. Charlie Davies, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo Network, previews Thursday's match and breaks down the U.S. chances for the rest of the tournament.

item.title: Ongoing protests in Albania over luxury tourism resort backed by Jared Kushner
item.description: There have been weeks of protests in Albania over a luxury tourism development backed by President Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner. The resort is set to rise in a protected area known for its natural beauty. Leigh Kiniry reports.

item.title: U.S. could make history in World Cup match against Turkey
item.description: The U.S. men's national team has already won their group, but they still have a chance to make history in their World Cup match against Turkey. A win on Thursday would mark the first time the team has won all three matches in the group stage. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, killing at least 164
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: Snap sued over rape of minor who met adult attacker via Snapchat
item.description: The parents of a girl who was raped when she was 12 years old by an adult stranger she connected to via Snapchat have sued its parent company, Snap, and the attacker, in Missouri state court.

item.title: Democrats to propose bill capping out-of-pocket Medicare costs for enrollees
item.description: Some Senate Democrats want to cap the amount beneficiaries in traditional Medicare have to pay toward care, but the move is expected to draw GOP opposition for potentially adding billions to Medicare costs.

item.title: Military requiring flu vaccines for recruits as Air Force base deals with outbreak
item.description: All military branches began requiring recruits to get flu vaccines earlier this month, an exception to Pete Hegseth's decision to lift the military's vaccine mandate, a Pentagon official said.

item.title: Espaillat's loss shakes up House Democrats, Hispanic caucus
item.description: Five-term incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat was defeated in the Democratic primary Tuesday night in a stunning upset by political newcomer and democratic socialist Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: How FIFA World Cup rules on extra time and tiebreakers work for 2026
item.description: Games are likely to be tied at the end of regulation at the 2026 World Cup, especially in the late stages of the tournament with a highly competitive field.

item.title: Bayer proposes $7.2 billion settlement to resolve weedkiller cases
item.description: Germany's Bayer has faced thousands of lawsuits after buying Roundup maker Monsanto in 2018 for $63 billion.
